sections.title: Capital Budgeting
sections.content: Capital budgeting involves evaluating investment projects to determine which ones to undertake. Key methods include NPV, IRR, and payback period.
keyPoints: IRR is the discount rate that makes NPV = 0
Payback period measures time to recover initial investment
NPV is preferred over IRR for mutually exclusive projects

practiceQuestions.question: A project with a positive NPV should be:
practiceQuestions.explanation: A positive NPV indicates the project will create value for shareholders and should be accepted.

practiceQuestions.question: The IRR is the discount rate that makes:
options: Cash flows equal to initial investment
practiceQuestions.explanation: By definition, IRR is the discount rate that makes the net present value of all cash flows equal to zero.

practiceQuestions.question: When NPV and IRR give conflicting rankings for mutually exclusive projects, which should be preferred?
practiceQuestions.explanation: NPV is preferred because it directly measures value creation and assumes reinvestment at the cost of capital, which is more realistic.

sections.title: Cost of Capital
sections.content: The cost of capital represents the opportunity cost of investing in a project. WACC is commonly used as the discount rate in capital budgeting.
keyPoints: WACC = weighted average of cost of debt and equity
Cost of equity > Cost of debt (due to tax shield)
Marginal cost of capital increases with more capital raised

practiceQuestions.question: The cost of equity is typically higher than the cost of debt because:
options: Equity holders have residual claims and higher risk
practiceQuestions.explanation: Equity holders bear more risk (residual claims, no guaranteed payments), so they require higher returns than debt holders.

sections.title: Leverage and Capital Structure
sections.content: Capital structure decisions involve choosing the mix of debt and equity financing. Modigliani-Miller theorem provides the theoretical foundation.
keyPoints: Financial leverage magnifies both gains and losses
Interest tax shield makes debt attractive
Financial distress costs limit debt usage
Optimal capital structure balances benefits and costs of debt

formulas.expression: Degree of Financial Leverage = EBIT / (EBIT - Interest)

practiceQuestions.question: According to Modigliani-Miller (with taxes), the value of a levered firm is:
practiceQuestions.explanation: With corporate taxes, the interest tax shield makes levered firms more valuable than unlevered firms by the present value of the tax shield.
